CAL-CENTRAL AIRLINE OFFICIALS CELEBRATE-There are many reasons for the
broad smiles worn by Valleyites Colonel C. C. and Mrs. Edna Sherman (center and
second from left) as they meet with advertising officials and press to observe new
speedy, luxury Martinliner operation in their intrastate operation. Shown. with this
man-wife airline combine are (left to right) Arch Keener, company advertising man-
ager; Mildred Fluent of ad agency, Calkins & Holden, Carlock, McClinon & Smith,
and Tom Self. Aviation Week.

Central Airlines
Buys New Planes
California Central Airlines, Cali-
fornia's pioneer
in
reduced
fare
commutter class intrastate flight
services, is partially replacing its
fleet with five Martin 202s, it is
reliably reported.
The 202s, acquired from North-
west Airlines, will have 44 seats,
two lounges and a buffet. Reason
for the change from DC-3 service
is
greater
speed,
comfort and
heavier
density loads
to
meet
growing travel demands, it is fur-
ther understood.
No starting date for the
new
MICHAEL
service has been announced.
